Metadata.name: WhatAisle
Metadata.title: WhatAisle — "What Aisle Is It In?" Answered for Grocery Stores
Metadata.description: WhatAisle lets grocery shoppers ask "what aisle is it in?" and get the exact shelf with a map — staff just snap shelf photos, shoppers search in English or Chinese. No app, no data entry.

hero.title: Shoppers ask “what aisle?” Your store answers: “Shelf B4.”
hero.description: Staff snap casual photos of shelves and your store grows a memory of what's where. Shoppers scan a QR code and search by typing, speaking, or taking a photo — in English or Chinese.
hero.demoAnswer: Yes — we've seen it on shelf B4, left side.

snap.title: Snap shelves as you walk
snap.description: Staff photograph shelves in passing — no product database, no data entry, ever. AI finds every product in the photo, reads packaging in English, Chinese, Korean or Japanese, and blurs faces before anything is stored.

memory.title: The memory grows
memory.description: Every snap adds products to your store's memory — deduplicated, named in English, Chinese and pinyin, pinned to a shelf code. The more you snap, the better the search.

ask.title: Shoppers ask, any way
ask.description: Shoppers scan the in-store QR code — nothing to install. They type, hold to talk, or photograph a package, in English or Chinese.

answer.title: The answer, on the map
answer.description: They get the exact shelf — not just an aisle — with the floor map highlighted so they can walk straight to it.
answer.demoAnswer: It should be on shelf B4 — left side.
answer.mapCaption: shelf B4 · highlighted for the shopper

tryScan.title: See what our AI reads on a shelf
tryScan.subtitle: Drop in one photo of a store shelf. We'll find every product on it — no sign-up needed.
tryScan.fileHint: JPG, PNG or HEIC · up to 15 MB
tryScan.resultHint: Sign up to scan whole aisles, name every product and make them searchable by your shoppers.

err.not_shelf: That doesn't look like a store shelf. Try a photo of stocked shelving.
err.not_legible: The labels are too blurry or far away to read. Get closer and try again.
err.too_large: That file is too big (max 15 MB).
err.invalid_image: We couldn't read that file. Try a regular photo (JPG, PNG, or HEIC).
err.rate_limited: Too many attempts — please try again tomorrow.
err.limit_reached: You've used all 3 free scans. Create a free account to keep going.
err.scan_failed: Something went wrong reading the shelf. Please try again.

scan.title: One QR code. Every shelf, answered.
scan.sub: Print it, stick it up, done. Shoppers scan and ask — your store answers the aisle. No app to install.
scan.caption: scan to find any product

seo.title: “What aisle is it in?” — every what aisle question, answered
seo.intro1: Every grocery store hears the same “what aisle” questions all day long: “What aisle is the chili sauce in?” “What aisle is the rice vinegar in?” WhatAisle turns every “what aisle” question into a self-serve answer. Shoppers scan a QR code at the entrance or the checkout counter, ask in their own words — in English or Chinese — and get the exact shelf, not just an aisle number, together with a floor map that highlights exactly where to walk.
seo.intro2: Unlike inventory systems that demand barcode scanning and constant data entry, WhatAisle builds its knowledge from casual shelf photos your staff already have time to take. There is no product database to maintain, no spreadsheet to update, and nothing for shoppers to install. The store’s memory of what sits on which shelf grows a little every time someone walks past with a phone — so the next time a shopper wonders what aisle the noodles are in, the answer is already waiting.

examples.title: The “what aisle” questions shoppers ask every day
examples.q1: What aisle is soy sauce in?
examples.q2: What aisle is rice vinegar in?
examples.q3: What aisle are instant noodles in?
examples.q4: What aisle is chili oil in?
examples.q5: What aisle is jasmine rice in?
examples.q6: What aisle is fish sauce in?
examples.q7: What aisle are frozen dumplings in?
examples.q8: What aisle is sesame oil in?
examples.q9: What aisle is oyster sauce in?
examples.q11: What aisle are rice cakes in?

owners.title: For grocery store owners
owners.p1: Staff walk the aisles and snap photos of shelves as part of their normal routine. Our vision AI detects every product in the photo, reads the packaging in English, Chinese, Korean or Japanese, and files each item under its shelf code. Faces are blurred automatically before anything is stored. The more photos your team takes, the more complete your store’s memory becomes — and the more often the next “what aisle is this in?” gets an instant answer.
owners.p2: The dashboard shows what shoppers searched for, which searches succeeded, and — most valuable of all — what people asked for that you don’t stock yet. When a shopper asks what aisle a product is in and the honest answer is “we don’t carry it”, that is a buying signal you would never hear about otherwise. WhatAisle collects those misses into a simple restock-and-source list you can act on every week.
owners.p3: Answering “what aisle?” by hand costs real staff time. Owners tell us the question interrupts stocking, checkout, and prep dozens of times a day — and every unanswered “what aisle” moment is a shopper one step closer to leaving empty-handed. Making the answer self-serve gives that time back.

shoppers.title: For shoppers
shoppers.p1: No app, no account, no learning curve. Scan the QR code, then type, speak, or photograph a package. Ask “what aisle is soy sauce in?” out loud, or paste a product name in Chinese — the answer comes back in seconds with the shelf code and a highlighted map. If the store hasn’t scanned that product yet, WhatAisle says so honestly instead of sending you down the wrong aisle. Every “what aisle” search gets a straight answer or an honest “not yet”.

multilingual.title: Built for multilingual neighborhood markets
multilingual.p1: Asian grocery stores and international markets field the “what aisle” question in more than one language. WhatAisle understands search queries in English and Chinese, matches pinyin and common misspellings, and reads packaging in four scripts. A shopper can ask the same “what aisle” question in Chinese about a product labeled in Korean and still get the right shelf — because the store’s memory keeps every name a product goes by.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: What aisle or which shelf — what does WhatAisle actually answer?
faq.a1: Both, but we aim for the shelf. A typical answer looks like “Shelf B4, left side”, with that shelf highlighted on your floor map. That is more precise than telling a shopper what aisle to wander down and hoping they spot the product.
faq.q2: How much work is it to set up?
faq.a2: Create your store, print the QR poster, and have staff photograph shelves aisle by aisle — most stores have a usable product memory within the first afternoon. A short walk-through video of your aisles is enough for us to draft your floor map, which you review and approve before it goes live.
faq.q3: Do shoppers need to download an app?
faq.a3: No. The QR code opens a web page in the phone’s browser. Shoppers ask what aisle something is in by typing, holding to talk, or snapping a photo of the packaging — nothing to install on either side.
faq.a4: Shoppers can search in English or Chinese, by text or voice. On the shelf side, the AI reads packaging text in English, Chinese, Korean and Japanese, so a multilingual store’s whole range stays searchable no matter what aisle it lives in.
faq.q5: What happens when a “what aisle” search finds nothing?
faq.a5: The shopper gets an honest “we haven’t seen it yet” plus close alternatives, and the miss lands in your insights — either as a reminder to scan that aisle, or as a signal that shoppers want something you don’t carry yet.
faq.a6: Yes. Faces are blurred before shelf photos are stored, shopper photos are used only to identify the product and are not kept, and searches are aggregated for insights rather than tied to individuals.
faq.a7: You can start on the free plan: scan shelves, publish your QR code, and answer real “what aisle” searches without paying anything. As your scan and search volume grows, paid plans lift the limits — full details are on the pricing page.

seo.outro: Whether shoppers ask “what aisle is it in?” at the entrance, at the checkout, or standing lost in the middle of the store, every “what aisle” moment should take seconds — not a staff hunt. Put your store’s shelves one scan away: print the QR poster, snap a few aisles this afternoon, and let WhatAisle answer the what aisle question for you.

Onboarding.title: Set up your store

Onboarding.handleHint: Letters, numbers, and hyphens only (3–30 characters).
Onboarding.permanenceWarning: This will be the permanent web address shoppers use for your store. It will be printed on your posters — choose carefully.

errors.format: Only letters, numbers, and hyphens — starting and ending with a letter or number.

permissionDenied.mic: Microphone access is off. You can type your search instead, or turn on the microphone in your browser settings.
permissionDenied.camera: Camera access is off. You can type your search instead, or turn on the camera in your browser settings.

results.rephrase: Try another name, a brand, or describe what it looks like.
results.degraded: Search is running in a simpler mode right now — results may be less precise.

video.title: Send us a store walk-through
video.subtitle: Film one lap of your store and we'll turn it into your floor map.
video.keepOpen: You can leave this page — the upload keeps going and picks up where it left off.
video.guide1: Walk one full lap of your store.
video.guide2: Point the camera at each aisle and its sign.
video.guide3: Go slow enough to read the shelf numbers.
video.received: Got it! We've received your video.
video.receivedNote: We'll draw your store map and let you know when it's ready. You can start scanning shelves right now — nothing waits on the map.
video.error: Upload failed. Please try again — it will resume where it stopped.
video.tooBig: That video is very large. It will upload in the background; keep this page open.

instructions.title: How to film your store
instructions.subtitle: The video becomes your product map — anything you skip will be missing from the map.

entrance.title: Start at the entrance
entrance.detail: Begin recording at the store entrance and film your way in.

everyAisle.title: Walk every aisle
everyAisle.detail: Cover the whole store — do not skip any aisle, corner, wall, or checkout area.

categorySigns.title: Capture category signs
categorySigns.detail: If an aisle has category signs, make sure each sign is clearly visible in the video.

steady.title: Move slowly and steadily
steady.detail: Keep the camera steady and shelf labels readable — walking pace, no rushing.

oneTake.title: One continuous take is best
oneTake.detail: A single video up to 5 GB (MP4/MOV, max 30 minutes) works best.

map.title: Your store map

map.published: Your map is live. Shoppers see it in search results.
map.returnPlaceholder: e.g. Aisle B4 and B5 are swapped

map.layoutUpdate: Shelves moved? Request a layout update
